articles.description: Moreangels Mbizah has blazed a trail in Zimbabwe as the first black African woman to found a conservation organisation in the country

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: LANDMARK RULING: Court orders woman to pay back all maintenance money after 0% DNA Test results
more.description: HWANGE – In a landmark ruling that has ignited widespread discussion across Zimbabwe, a court in Dete – a small town along the Bulawayo-Victoria Falls highway – has ordered a woman to reimburse her former partner US$2,500 in maintenance payments after DNA evidence conclusively proved he was not the biological father of the child. This [...] The post LANDMARK RULING: Court orders woman to pay back all maintenance money after 0% DNA Test results first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Loyalty for sale — how patronage networks captured the Zimbabwean state
more.description: A transactional political economy in Zimbabwe fosters corruption and dependency, as loyalty to power trumps democratic principles, disenfranchising everyday citizens.

more.title: Rock art, dance and ritual: what we learned from paintings in Zimbabwe
more.description: Rock paintings are found throughout Zimbabwe. They were made during the last 10,000 years by hunter gatherer groups and later by farming communities.

more.title: Mozambique-Zimbabwe Rail Line Reopens After Four-month Closure
more.description: Source: Mozambique-Zimbabwe Rail Line Reopens After Four-month Closure ⋆ Pindula News The National Railways of Zimbabwe (NRZ) has confirmed that major repair work on the Chicualacuala-Maputo rail line is now complete. The line has been closed since mid-January. That forced the suspension of freight to and from the Port of Maputo into Zimbabwe and other [...] The post Mozambique-Zimbabwe Rail Line Reopens After Four-month Closure appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ZEC Sets 20 June For Chiredzi And Tsholotsho By-elections
more.description: By-elections will be held in four rural wards on 20 June, the Zimbabwe Electoral Commission (ZEC) has announced.

more.title: Let us value the prevailing peace — VP Chiwenga

more.title: Govt begins process of blending diesel amid fuel cost pressures
more.description: Source: Govt begins process of blending diesel amid fuel cost pressures – herald Debra Matabvu ZIMBABWE has begun formal research and trials into blending diesel with ethanol, marking a potentially significant shift as the Government continues to implement measures to cushion consumers from rising fuel costs, reduce import dependence and shield the economy from global [...] The post Govt begins process of blending diesel amid fuel cost pressures appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .
